quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Triglyceride"?

options.text: The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success
Three fatty acids linked to glycerol via ester bonds; fats (saturated, solid at room temp) and oils (unsaturated, liquid at room temp)
A 10-step metabolic pathway in the cytoplasm that converts glucose (6C) into 2 pyruvate (3C), producing 2 net ATP and 2 NADH
ATP production driven by the proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane; H⁺ flows through ATP synthase from lumen to stroma

quiz_questions.explanation: "Triglyceride" is defined as: Three fatty acids linked to glycerol via ester bonds; fats (saturated, solid at room temp) and oils (unsaturated, liquid at room temp). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Phospholipid"?

options.text: A structural polysaccharide in plant cell walls made of β-glucose linked by β-1,4 glycosidic bonds; forms rigid, indigestible fibers
Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)
Amphipathic lipid with a hydrophilic phosphate head and two hydrophobic fatty acid tails; forms the cell membrane bilayer
The end product of a metabolic pathway inhibits an early enzyme in the pathway, preventing overproduction (e.g., isoleucine inhibiting threonine deaminase)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Phospholipid" is defined as: Amphipathic lipid with a hydrophilic phosphate head and two hydrophobic fatty acid tails; forms the cell membrane bilayer. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Saturated fatty acid"?

options.text: The rate at which producers store energy as biomass minus their own respiratory losses; NPP = GPP - respiration
A fatty acid with no C=C double bonds; straight chain allows tight packing, resulting in solid fats at room temperature
Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)
The pathway by which a chemical element (C, N, P, H₂O) moves through the biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem

quiz_questions.explanation: "Saturated fatty acid" is defined as: A fatty acid with no C=C double bonds; straight chain allows tight packing, resulting in solid fats at room temperature. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Unsaturated fatty acid"?

options.text: Failure of chromosomes to separate properly during meiosis, resulting in gametes with abnormal chromosome numbers (aneuploidy)
The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success
A fatty acid with one or more C=C double bonds creating kinks; prevents tight packing, liquid at room temperature
Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Unsaturated fatty acid" is defined as: A fatty acid with one or more C=C double bonds creating kinks; prevents tight packing, liquid at room temperature. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Steroid"?

options.text: The rate at which producers store energy as biomass minus their own respiratory losses; NPP = GPP - respiration
A lipid with four fused carbon rings; includes cholesterol (membrane fluidity), testosterone, estrogen, and cortisol
Changes in chromatin structure that affect gene accessibility: histone acetylation opens chromatin (activating), deacetylation compacts it (silencing)
The first photosystem in the light reactions; absorbs 680nm light, splits water, and passes excited electrons to the electron transport chain

quiz_questions.explanation: "Steroid" is defined as: A lipid with four fused carbon rings; includes cholesterol (membrane fluidity), testosterone, estrogen, and cortisol. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Three fatty acids linked to glycerol via ester bonds; fats (saturated, solid at room temp) and oils (unsaturated, liquid at room temp)"?

options.text: Carrying capacity (K)
Law of Segregation
Triglyceride
microRNA (miRNA)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Triglyceride".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Amphipathic lipid with a hydrophilic phosphate head and two hydrophobic fatty acid tails; forms the cell membrane bilayer"?

options.text: Nucleus
Mutagen
Leading strand
Phospholipid

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Phospholipid".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A fatty acid with no C=C double bonds; straight chain allows tight packing, resulting in solid fats at room temperature"?

options.text: Electron transport chain
Ligand
Saturated fatty acid
Independent assortment

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Saturated fatty acid".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A fatty acid with one or more C=C double bonds creating kinks; prevents tight packing, liquid at room temperature"?

options.text: Unsaturated fatty acid
Genetic drift
Incomplete dominance
microRNA (miRNA)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Unsaturated fatty acid".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A lipid with four fused carbon rings; includes cholesterol (membrane fluidity), testosterone, estrogen, and cortisol"?

options.text: Oxidative phosphorylation
Steroid
Aerobic vs. anaerobic
Linked genes

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Steroid".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Lipids is accurate?

options.text: Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.
Community Ecology examines interactions between species in ecological communities.
Lipids are hydrophobic or amphipathic molecules including fats, phospholipids, and steroids.
Population viability analysis (PVA) estimates extinction probability for small populations considering demographic and environmental stochasticity.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Lipids as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Lipids?

options.text: Triglycerides (fats and oils) store energy (9 kcal/g) through ester bonds between glycerol and three fatty acids.
Translation is the process by which ribosomes synthesize proteins from mRNA templates.
The enzyme RuBisCO catalyzes carbon fixation (CO₂ + RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA), making it the most abundant protein on Earth.
Adaptive radiation (rapid diversification from a single ancestor into multiple ecological niches) often follows mass extinctions or colonization of new environments.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Lipids as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Lipids?

options.text: Evolution and Evidence establishes that populations change over time through multiple converging lines of evidence.
Repair mechanisms include base excision repair (BER) for damaged bases, nucleotide excision repair (NER) for bulky lesions, and mismatch repair for replication errors.

The three stages—reception (ligand binding), transduction (signal relay/amplification through phosphorylation cascades), and response (gene expression, enzyme activation, or cell movement)—allow cells to respond to their environment.
Phospholipids form the basis of cell membrane bilayers due to their amphipathic nature (hydrophilic head, hydrophobic tails).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Lipids as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Lipids?

options.text: Saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acids differ in the presence of C=C double bonds, affecting fluidity and melting point.
Biotechnology Applications encompasses the tools and techniques used to manipulate DNA.
The Knudson two-hit hypothesis explains that both copies of a tumor suppressor must be inactivated for cancer to develop.
The three modes of natural selection are directional (shifts the mean), stabilizing (reduces variation around the mean), and disruptive (favors extremes).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Lipids as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Lipids?

options.text: Parsimony (fewest evolutionary changes) and maximum likelihood methods are used to determine the most probable tree topology.
Lipids are hydrophobic or amphipathic molecules including fats, phospholipids, and steroids.
Competitive inhibitors resemble the substrate and compete for the active site, while noncompetitive inhibitors bind elsewhere and reduce catalytic activity.
Minimum viable population (MVP), effective population size (Ne), and genetic rescue inform conservation management.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Lipids as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Lipids?

options.text: Trans fats (partially hydrogenated oils) increasing cardiovascular disease risk by raising LDL and lowering HDL cholesterol levels
Capillary action in plant xylem vessels transporting water against gravity from roots to leaves through cohesion-adhesion-tension
Antibiotic targeting of bacterial ribosomes: tetracycline blocks the A-site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase—both exploit differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes
HPV (human papillomavirus) causing cervical cancer by producing proteins that inactivate p53 and Rb tumor suppressors, disabling cell cycle checkpoints

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Lipids.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Lipids?

options.text: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing using guide RNA complementary base pairing to target specific DNA sequences for precise genetic modification
Metabolic interconnections: Krebs cycle intermediates serve as precursors for amino acid synthesis, fatty acid synthesis, and gluconeogenesis
Cell membrane fluidity regulation through cholesterol insertion between phospholipids and adjustment of saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acid ratios
Lead poisoning: lead (Pb²⁺) replaces zinc in enzymes, acting as a noncompetitive inhibitor and disrupting nervous system, kidney, and blood cell function

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Lipids.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Lipids applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration
Endosymbiotic theory evidence: mitochondria and chloroplasts have double membranes, own circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, and replicate independently
Myelin sheaths (lipid-rich) insulating nerve axons to increase signal transmission speed from 2 m/s to over 100 m/s
Capillary action in plant xylem vessels transporting water against gravity from roots to leaves through cohesion-adhesion-tension

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Lipids.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Lipids?

options.text: ABO blood type compatibility in transfusions determined by codominant Iᴬ and Iᴮ alleles and recessive i allele (3 alleles, 6 genotypes, 4 phenotypes)
Exercise physiology: transitioning from aerobic to anaerobic respiration during intense activity when O₂ delivery cannot meet demand
Trans fats (partially hydrogenated oils) increasing cardiovascular disease risk by raising LDL and lowering HDL cholesterol levels
Forensic genetics using Mendelian inheritance patterns to establish paternity or identify family relationships from DNA evidence

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Lipids.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Lipids in practice?

options.text: Cancer immunotherapy using checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1, anti-CTLA-4) to re-enable the immune system's ability to attack cancer cells
Cell membrane fluidity regulation through cholesterol insertion between phospholipids and adjustment of saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acid ratios
Cyanide poisoning blocking Complex IV of the ETC, preventing electron transfer to O₂ and halting all aerobic ATP production
Insulin signaling pathway: insulin binding to its receptor triggers a kinase cascade leading to glucose transporter (GLUT4) insertion into cell membranes

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Lipids.
